standard_description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: Italics, underlines, and quotation marks
khan_exercises.description: Learn how to use italics, underlines, and quotation marks when writing titles of books, movies, songs, etc.
topic_path: Punctuation: the colon, semicolon, and more

items.question_content:
**Choose the correct formatting based on the information about the title/work in this sentence.**

 \_\_\_\_\_\_ is one of my favorite adventure novels.

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints:
Remember, there are three types of formatting used for formal titles: **italics, underlines,** and **quotation marks.**

**Italics** and **underlines** are used for "big" titles: books, albums, tv shows, etc.

**Quotation marks** are used for "small" titles: short stories, songs, tv episodes, etc.

So, while a poem would take quotation marks, a *book of poetry* would take italics or be underlined.

The sentence tells us we're naming a novel, so we should use either italics or an underline:

>***The Hobbit***

options.explanation:
Remember, there are three types of formatting used for formal titles: **italics, underlines,** and **quotation marks.**

**Italics** and **underlines** are used for "big" titles: books, albums, tv shows, etc.

**Quotation marks** are used for "small" titles: short stories, songs, tv episodes, etc.

So, while a poem would take quotation marks, a *book of poetry* would take italics or be underlined.
options.showPrompt: What are the formatting types again?

khan_exercises.title: Introduction to parentheses
khan_exercises.description: Parentheses are used to set off extra information from a sentence, show an interruption, or indicate a reference. Practice identifying their uses in this exercise!
topic_path: Punctuation: the colon, semicolon, and more

items.question_content:
**What are the parentheses doing in this sentence?**

>The vampire (who had, moments ago, turned into a bat) was distressed to overhear what the other monsters thought of him.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Parentheses** separate a piece of grammatically unnecessary information from the rest of the sentence.

Parentheses can:   

* add definitions, extra details, or other useful information
* allow an author to make a comment on what's happening
* show interruptions within speech or dialogue
* provide a reference to the source of information in the sentence

What type of information is contained within parentheses in this sentence?
These parentheses are **providing extra explanation**.

items.question_content:
**What are the parentheses doing in this sentence?**

>Ingmar Bergman (a famous Swedish filmmaker) made one of my favorite movies – Persona.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Parentheses** separate a piece of grammatically unnecessary information from the rest of the sentence.

Parentheses can:   

* add definitions, extra details, or other useful information
* allow an author to make a comment on what's happening
* show interruptions within speech or dialogue
* provide a reference to the source of information in the sentence

What type of information is contained within parentheses in this sentence?
These parentheses are **providing extra explanation**.

choices.content: Showing an opinion of the writer

items.question_content:
**What are the parentheses doing in this sentence?**

>The Mongolian folk dancer (who was balancing a bowl of milk on her head) skillfully moved through her poses.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Parentheses** separate a piece of grammatically unnecessary information from the rest of the sentence.

Parentheses can:   

* add definitions, extra details, or other useful information
* allow an author to make a comment on what's happening
* show interruptions within speech or dialogue
* provide a reference to the source of information in the sentence

What type of information is contained within parentheses in this sentence?
These parentheses are **providing additional details**.

items.question_content:
**What are the parentheses doing in this sentence?**

>Javier (in spite of his reservations) decided to go to the house party.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Parentheses** separate a piece of grammatically unnecessary information from the rest of the sentence.

Parentheses can:   

* add definitions, extra details, or other useful information
* allow an author to make a comment on what's happening
* show interruptions within speech or dialogue
* provide a reference to the source of information in the sentence

What type of information is contained within parentheses in this sentence?
These parentheses are **showing a comment the writer is making**.

choices.content: Showing a comment the writer is making

items.question_content:
**What are the parentheses doing in this sentence?**

>“And then I said, ‘Is that all the goats you’ve got?’ (laughter).”

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Parentheses** separate a piece of grammatically unnecessary information from the rest of the sentence.

Parentheses can:   

* add definitions, extra details, or other useful information
* allow an author to make a comment on what's happening
* show interruptions within speech or dialogue
* provide a reference to the source of information in the sentence

What type of information is contained within parentheses in this sentence?
These parentheses are **showing an interruption in dialogue**.

choices.content: Showing a comment the author is making
